info.name: Second Brain API — Complete Collection
info.description:
Complete REST API collection for the Second Brain backend.

Stack: Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL
Auth: JWT (httpOnly cookies) + Bearer token + RBAC
Modules: Auth, Notes, Folders, Tags

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` in the environment (default: http://localhost:5000/api/v1)
2. Register or login — accessToken & refreshToken are set as httpOnly cookies automatically
3. Copy the `accessToken` value from the login response cookie into the `accessToken` environment variable to use Bearer auth on protected routes

variable.description: Base URL for all API requests
JWT access token — populated automatically by login/register test scripts
JWT refresh token — populated automatically by login/register test scripts

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints — registration, login, logout, token refresh, and password reset via OTP.

exec: // Note: tokens are set as httpOnly cookies automatically

request.description:
Creates a new user account. Returns user data and sets httpOnly cookies (accessToken, refreshToken).

**Validation:**
- name: min 2 chars
- email: valid email format
- password: min 6 chars

**Response 201:**
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": { "user": { "id": "uuid", "name": "...", "email": "...", "role": "user" } }
}
```

exec: // Extract accessToken from Set-Cookie header if needed

request.description: Revokes the refresh token from the DB and clears the refreshToken cookie. The accessToken cookie must be cleared client-side or will expire naturally (15 min).
Issues new access + refresh token pair using the refreshToken cookie (token rotation). The old refresh token is revoked.

Sends a 6-digit OTP to the user's email (via Resend). Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists (prevents email enumeration).

**Rate limited:** 10 req / 15 min per IP. Cooldown of 60s between OTP requests per user. Max 5 OTPs per hour per user.

OTP expires in 10 minutes.

response.name: 200 OK (Always)
429 Rate Limited

request.description:
Verifies a 6-digit OTP. The `type` field must be `forgot_password` or `signup`.

On success, returns `userId` which can be used to confirm identity before resetting password.

**Rate limited:** 10 req / 15 min per IP.

Resets password using the OTP. Re-verifies the OTP internally. Prevents reuse of the same password. Revokes all active sessions on success (forces re-login everywhere).

**Rate limited:** 10 req / 15 min per IP.

item.name: 📝 Notes
item.description: Full CRUD for notes, plus search, archive toggle, batch operations, and folder assignment.

request.description:
Creates a new note for the authenticated user. Optionally accepts a `tags` array — tags are created if they don't exist.

**Validation:**
- title: required, max 255 chars
- content: required, max 100,000 chars
- tags: optional, array of strings (max 20, each max 50 chars)

**Returns:** Note object with tags array.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description:
Returns paginated notes for the authenticated user, each with tags. Excludes archived notes by default.

**Query params:**
- `page` (default: 1)
- `limit` (default: 20, max: 100)
- `includeArchived` (default: false)

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Fetches all notes including archived ones.
Returns a single note with its full tag list. Returns 404 if not found or doesn't belong to the user.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Partially updates a note. At least one of `title` or `content` must be provided.

response.name: 400 Nothing to Update

request.description: Permanently deletes a note. Associated note_tags records are cascade-deleted.

Full-text search across note titles and content using PostgreSQL `to_tsvector`. Results are ranked by relevance (`ts_rank`).

**Query params:**
- `query` (required, min 2 chars)
- `page`, `limit`

response.name: 400 Query Too Short

request.description: Returns non-archived notes created within the last N days. Default: 7. Max: 30.

query.description: Number of days to look back (1–30)

request.description: Toggles the `is_archived` flag on a note. Call again to unarchive. No body required.
Moves a note into a folder. Set `folderId` to `null` to remove from folder (move to uncategorized).
Removes a note from its current folder by setting folderId to null.

Permanently deletes up to 100 notes in one request. Only deletes notes owned by the authenticated user.

**Validation:**
- `noteIds`: array of valid UUIDs (min 1, max 100)
Archives or unarchives up to 100 notes. Set `archive: false` to unarchive.

item.name: 📁 Folders
item.description: Folder management for organizing notes. Folder names are unique per user (case-insensitive). Deleting a folder moves its notes to uncategorized (folder_id = NULL).

request.description:
Creates a new folder. Name is unique per user (case-insensitive — 'Work' and 'work' are the same).

**Validation:**
- name: required, 1–255 chars

response.name: 409 Duplicate Name

request.description: Returns all folders for the authenticated user, each with a `note_count` of non-archived notes.
Returns a folder with its stats (note_count of non-archived notes).
Renames a folder. Still validates for uniqueness (excluding the current folder).
Deletes a folder. All notes inside the folder have their `folder_id` set to NULL (moved to uncategorized). Notes are NOT deleted.
Returns paginated notes belonging to a specific folder (non-archived only). Also returns the folder object.

item.name: 🏷️ Tags
item.description: Tag management with many-to-many note associations. Tags are global (shared across users) but usage is tracked per user. Tags are find-or-created on attach.

request.description:
Creates a tag or returns the existing one (find-or-create pattern).

**Validation:**
- name: 1–50 chars, alphanumeric + spaces + hyphens + underscores only
Returns all tags with usage_count, sorted by most-used. Supports optional search filter.
Returns only tags that the authenticated user has actually used on their notes, with usage count.

query.description: Search prefix (min 1 char, returns top 10 matches)

request.description: Returns up to 10 matching tags for autocomplete UI. Results sorted by usage_count.
Returns a single tag by its UUID, with usage_count.
Deletes a tag only if it is not attached to any notes. Returns 400 if the tag is in use.

response.name: 400 Tag In Use

request.description:
Attaches one or more tags to a note by name. Tags are created if they don't exist (find-or-create). Duplicate attachments are silently ignored.

**Returns:** Full updated tags array for the note.

**Validation:**
- tags: array, min 1, max 20
- each tag: 1–50 chars, alphanumeric + spaces + hyphens + underscores
Removes a specific tag from a note. Does NOT delete the tag itself.

Atomically replaces ALL tags on a note. Removes old tags and attaches new ones in a single DB transaction. Use an empty array `[]` to clear all tags.

**Returns:** Updated tags array.

query.description: false = ANY tag match, true = ALL tags must be present

request.description:
Returns notes that have ANY of the specified tags. Set `matchAll=true` to require ALL tags to be present on the note.

**Body:**
- `tagIds`: array of tag UUIDs (1–20)

**Query:**
- `matchAll`: `true` = AND logic, `false` = OR logic (default: false)
Returns notes that have ALL of the specified tags (AND logic).

item.name: ✅ Validation Tests
item.description: Pre-built requests to test validation error responses. All should return 4xx.

request.description: Calls a protected route without any authentication cookie or token.
Public health check endpoint. Should return 200 with a welcome message.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: Welcome to the Second Brain API
